sentences.sentence: A few years ago, Fred DeJesus from Brooklyn, New York became the first umpire in a minor league baseball game to use something called the Automated Ball-Strike System (ABS), often referred to as the 'robo-umpire'.
sentences.translation: 几年前，来自纽约布鲁克林的Fred DeJesus成为小联盟棒球比赛中首位使用自动好球系统（ABS）的裁判，这套系统常被称为“机器人裁判”。

structure.text: Fred DeJesus from Brooklyn, New York
to use something called the Automated Ball-Strike System (ABS)

difficult_points: to use 修饰 first umpire，说明他成为首位做什么的裁判。
called 与 referred to as 都是过去分词后置修饰系统。

sentences.sentence: Instead of making any judgments himself about a strike*, DeJesus had decisions fed to him through an earpiece, connected to a modified missile-tracking system.
sentences.translation: DeJesus不再亲自判断好球，而是通过连接改装导弹追踪系统的耳机接收判定。

structure.text: Instead of making any judgments himself about a strike
had decisions fed to him through an earpiece
connected to a modified missile-tracking system

difficult_points: Instead of 后接动名词，表示替代方式。
had decisions fed to him 是 have + 宾语 + 过去分词，使判定被传给他。

sentences.sentence: The contraption looked like a large black pizza box with one glowing green eye; it was mounted above the press stand.
sentences.translation: 这个装置看起来像一个带着发光绿眼的大黑色披萨盒；它安装在记者席上方。

structure.text: looked like a large black pizza box with one glowing green eye
was mounted above the press stand

difficult_points: look like 是系动词短语，with 短语补充外观。
分号连接两个独立分句，was mounted 是被动语态。

sentences.sentence: Major League Baseball (MLB), who had commissioned the system, wanted human umpires to announce the calls, just as they would have done in the past.
sentences.translation: 委托开发该系统的美国职业棒球大联盟希望由人类裁判宣布判罚，就像过去一样。

structure.text: wanted human umpires to announce the calls
just as they would have done in the past

difficult_points: who 修饰 MLB；wanted + 宾语 + to do 表示希望某人做某事。
would have done 中 done 代替 announce the calls，表示过去惯例。

sentences.sentence: When the first pitch came in, a recorded voice told DeJesus it was a strike.
sentences.translation: 第一球投来时，录音声音告诉DeJesus这是好球。

structure.text: When the first pitch came in

difficult_points: tell someone + 宾语从句表示告诉某人某事。
it 指第一球，was a strike 是录音判定内容。

sentences.sentence: Previously, calling a strike was a judgment call on the part of the umpire.
sentences.translation: 以前，判定好球是裁判的判断。

structure.text: a judgment call on the part of the umpire

difficult_points: calling a strike 是动名词作主语。
on the part of 表示由裁判作出。

sentences.sentence: Even if the batter does not hit the ball, a pitch that passes through the 'strike zone' (an imaginary zone about seventeen inches wide, stretching from the batter's knees to the middle of his chest) is considered a strike.
sentences.translation: 即使击球手没有击中球，只要投球穿过“好球区”（一个约17英寸宽、从击球手膝盖延伸到胸部中部的想象区域），就被视为好球。

structure.text: Even if the batter does not hit the ball
a pitch that passes through the 'strike zone'
an imaginary zone about seventeen inches wide, stretching from the batter's knees to the middle of his chest

difficult_points: that 修饰 pitch；Even if 从句让步说明击球与否不影响判定。
stretching 分词描述好球区范围，is considered 是被动。

sentences.sentence: During that first game, when DeJesus announced calls, there was no heckling and no shouted disagreement.
sentences.translation: 在那场首场比赛中，DeJesus宣布判罚时，没有嘘声，也没有高声反对。

structure.text: there was no heckling and no shouted disagreement

difficult_points: when 从句嵌在时间状语中；there was 结构的真正主语是两个 no 短语。
heckling 指观众起哄嘲弄。

sentences.sentence: Nobody said a word.
sentences.translation: 没有人说一句话。
difficult_points: nobody 是单数不定代词，谓语用 said。
a word 表示一句话，强调完全沉默。

sentences.sentence: For a hundred and fifty years or so, the strike zone has been the game's animating force—countless arguments between a team's manager and the umpire have taken place over its boundaries and whether a ball had crossed through it.
sentences.translation: 大约150年来，好球区一直是比赛的活力来源——球队经理与裁判围绕其边界以及球是否穿过它发生过无数争论。

structure.text: For a hundred and fifty years or so
has been the game's animating force
countless arguments between a team's manager and the umpire have taken place over its boundaries and whether a ball had crossed through it
whether a ball had crossed through it

difficult_points: 现在完成时 has been 表示持续至今；for...or so 表示大约。
whether 从句作 over 的并列对象，it 指 strike zone。

sentences.sentence: The rules of play have evolved in various stages.
sentences.translation: 比赛规则经历了多个阶段的演变。
difficult_points: 现在完成时说明规则从过去逐步演变至今。
of play 修饰 rules，表示比赛规则。

sentences.sentence: Today, everyone knows that you may scream your disagreement in an umpire's face, but you must never shout personal abuse at them or touch them.
sentences.translation: 如今人人都知道，可以当面高喊反对意见，但绝不能对裁判进行人身辱骂或触碰他们。

structure.text: that you may scream your disagreement in an umpire's face, but you must never shout personal abuse at them or touch them

difficult_points: that 从句中 may 与 must never 对比允许和禁止。
or 连接 shout 与 touch 两个动词，them 指 umpires。

sentences.sentence: That's a no-no.
sentences.translation: 那是绝对不允许的。
difficult_points: a no-no 是口语表达，表示禁忌或不允许的事。
That 指前句辱骂或触碰裁判的行为。

sentences.sentence: When the robo-umpires came, however, the arguments stopped.
sentences.translation: 然而，机器人裁判出现后，争论停止了。
difficult_points: When 从句说明争论停止的时间。
the arguments 指此前围绕好球区的争论。

sentences.sentence: During the first robo-umpire season, players complained about some strange calls.
sentences.translation: 在机器人裁判使用的第一个赛季，球员抱怨过一些奇怪的判罚。
difficult_points: complain about 后接抱怨对象；calls 指判罚。
during 短语限定抱怨发生时期。

sentences.sentence: In response, MLB decided to tweak the dimensions of the zone, and the following year the consensus was that ABS is profoundly consistent.
sentences.translation: 作为回应，MLB决定微调好球区尺寸；第二年，大家一致认为ABS极其稳定。

structure.text: MLB decided to tweak the dimensions of the zone
and the following year the consensus was that ABS is profoundly consistent

difficult_points: decide to tweak 表示决定微调；that 从句说明 consensus。
the following year 指下一赛季，ABS is... 使用一般现在时概括性能。

sentences.sentence: MLB says the device is near-perfect, precise to within fractions of an inch.
sentences.translation: MLB表示该设备近乎完美，精确到几分之一英寸以内。

structure.text: precise to within fractions of an inch

difficult_points: says 后省略 that；precise 短语补充说明设备精度。
within fractions of an inch 表示误差极小。

sentences.sentence: "It'll reduce controversy in the game, and be good for the game," says Rob Manfred, who is Commissioner for MLB.
sentences.translation: “它会减少比赛中的争议，对比赛有好处。”MLB专员Rob Manfred说。

structure.text: It'll reduce controversy in the game, and be good for the game

difficult_points: and 后 be good 与 reduce 并列，共用 will。
who 修饰 Rob Manfred，补充其职务。

sentences.sentence: But the question is whether controversy is worth reducing, or whether it is the sign of a human hand.
sentences.translation: 但问题在于，争议是否值得减少，或者它是否正是人类参与的标志。

structure.text: whether controversy is worth reducing, or whether it is the sign of a human hand

difficult_points: 两个 whether 从句由 or 并列，提出两种可能。
be worth doing 表示值得做；human hand 比喻人为作用。

sentences.sentence: A human, at least, yells back.
sentences.translation: 至少，人类会吼回去。
difficult_points: at least 插入表示最低限度的优点。
yell back 形象地表示回应争议。

sentences.sentence: When I spoke with Frank Viola, a coach for a North Carolina team, he said that ABS works as designed, but that it was also unforgiving and pedantic, almost legalistic.
sentences.translation: 我与北卡罗来纳州一支球队的教练Frank Viola交谈时，他说ABS按设计运行，但也不留情面、过于拘泥，几乎像法律条文一样。

structure.text: When I spoke with Frank Viola, a coach for a North Carolina team
that ABS works as designed, but that it was also unforgiving and pedantic, almost legalistic

difficult_points: said 后并列两个 that 从句；works as designed 表示按设计运行。
unforgiving、pedantic 与 legalistic 并列描述机器的僵硬。

sentences.sentence: "Manfred is a lawyer," Viola noted.
sentences.translation: “Manfred是律师。”Viola指出。
difficult_points: 引语中的系表结构直接说明身份；noted 是插入式引述动词。
上下文暗示律师思维可能导致规则过于严格。

sentences.sentence: Some pitchers have complained that, compared with a human's, the robot's strike zone seems too precise.
sentences.translation: 一些投手抱怨，与人类裁判相比，机器人的好球区似乎过于精确。

structure.text: that, compared with a human's, the robot's strike zone seems too precise

difficult_points: a human's 省略了 strike zone，表示人类裁判的好球区。
too precise 表示精确到缺乏弹性，而非单纯优点。

sentences.sentence: Viola was once a major-league player himself.
sentences.translation: Viola自己曾是大联盟球员。
difficult_points: himself 强调 Viola 本人有球员经历。
once 表示过去身份。

sentences.sentence: When he was pitching, he explained, umpires rewarded skill.
sentences.translation: 他解释说，在他投球时，裁判会奖励技巧。
difficult_points: When 从句使用过去进行时，表示当时正在投球。
he explained 插入主句，后面是解释内容。

sentences.sentence: "Throw it where you aimed, and it would be a strike, even if it was an inch or two outside.
sentences.translation: “把球投到你瞄准的位置，即使偏出一两英寸，也会被判为好球。

structure.text: and it would be a strike
even if it was an inch or two outside

difficult_points: 祈使句后接 and 表示条件结果；where 从句说明投球位置。
even if 让步说明即使略偏也会被认可。

sentences.sentence: There was a dialogue between pitcher and umpire."
sentences.translation: 投手与裁判之间存在对话。

structure.text: a dialogue between pitcher and umpire

difficult_points: There was 的真正主语是 a dialogue。
between pitcher and umpire 表示双方互动。

sentences.sentence: The executive tasked with running the experiment for MLB is Morgan Sword, who's in charge of baseball operations.
sentences.translation: 负责为MLB运行实验的高管是Morgan Sword，他主管棒球运营。

structure.text: The executive tasked with running the experiment for MLB
tasked with running the experiment for MLB
who's in charge of baseball operations

difficult_points: tasked with 后接动名词，修饰 executive；who 修饰 Morgan Sword。
be in charge of 表示负责。

sentences.sentence: According to Sword, ABS was part of a larger project to make baseball more exciting since executives are terrified of losing younger fans, as has been the case with horse racing and boxing.
sentences.translation: Sword说，ABS是让棒球更有趣的更大项目的一部分，因为管理层害怕失去年轻球迷，赛马和拳击已经出现了这种情况。

structure.text: ABS was part of a larger project to make baseball more exciting
since executives are terrified of losing younger fans
as has been the case with horse racing and boxing

difficult_points: to make baseball more exciting 修饰 project，since 引出原因。
be terrified of 后接动名词 losing；as has been the case 指类似先例。

sentences.sentence: He explains how they began the process by asking fans what version of baseball they found most exciting.
sentences.translation: 他解释了他们如何通过询问球迷最喜欢哪种棒球形式来开始这一过程。

structure.text: how they began the process by asking fans what version of baseball they found most exciting
by asking fans what version of baseball they found most exciting
what version of baseball they found most exciting

difficult_points: how 引导 explains 的宾语从句；by asking 说明开始方式。
what 从句作 asking 的宾语，they found most exciting 修饰 version。

sentences.sentence: The results showed that everyone wanted more action: more hits, more defense, more baserunning.
sentences.translation: 结果显示，大家都想要更多比赛行动：更多安打、防守和跑垒。

structure.text: more hits, more defense, more baserunning

difficult_points: that 从句说明调查结果；冒号后列举 action 的具体内容。
more hits 等为不可数/可数名词并列。

sentences.sentence: This type of baseball essentially hasn't existed since the 1960s, when the hundred-mile-an-hour fastball, which is difficult to hit and control, entered the game.
sentences.translation: 这种棒球风格自20世纪60年代以来基本不存在了，当时每小时100英里的快速球进入比赛，而这种球很难击打和控制。

structure.text: when the hundred-mile-an-hour fastball, which is difficult to hit and control, entered the game
which is difficult to hit and control

difficult_points: since 与现在完成时表示从60年代持续到现在不存在。
which 修饰 fastball；to hit and control 并列不定式。

sentences.sentence: It flattened the game into strikeouts, walks, and home runs—a type of play lacking much action.
sentences.translation: 它把比赛压缩成三振、保送和本垒打——一种缺乏太多行动的比赛方式。

structure.text: flattened the game into strikeouts, walks, and home runs
a type of play lacking much action

difficult_points: flatten A into B 表示把比赛简化成几类结果。
lacking 分词修饰 play，说明比赛缺少变化。

sentences.sentence: Sword's team brainstormed potential fixes.
sentences.translation: Sword的团队集思广益，寻找可能的解决办法。
difficult_points: brainstorm 作动词表示集思广益。
potential fixes 指可能修正规则的方法。

expressions.meaning_zh: 集思广益寻找解决办法

sentences.sentence: Any rule that existed, they talked about changing—from changing the bats to changing the geometry of the field.
sentences.translation: 他们讨论改变任何现有规则，从更换球棒到改变场地几何形状。

structure.text: from changing the bats to changing the geometry of the field

difficult_points: Any rule 置于句首作前置宾语；that 修饰 rule。
talk about 后接动名词 changing，from...to... 列范围。

sentences.sentence: But while all of these were ruled out as potential fixes, ABS was seen as a perfect vehicle for change.
sentences.translation: 但是，虽然这些方案都被排除为可能的修正办法，ABS却被视为改变的完美工具。

structure.text: while all of these were ruled out as potential fixes
ABS was seen as a perfect vehicle for change

difficult_points: while 引导让步从句；were ruled out 是被动排除。
be seen as 表示被视为，vehicle 为比喻工具。

sentences.sentence: According to Sword, once you get the technology right, you can load any strike zone you want into the system.
sentences.translation: Sword说，一旦技术调试正确，就可以把想要的任何好球区载入系统。

structure.text: once you get the technology right
you can load any strike zone you want into the system

difficult_points: get the technology right 是使役结构，表示把技术调试好。
you want 修饰 strike zone，省略了关系代词。

sentences.sentence: "It might be a triangle, or a blob, or something shaped like Texas.
sentences.translation: “它可以是三角形、团块形，或是德州形状的东西。”

structure.text: a triangle, or a blob, or something shaped like Texas

difficult_points: might be 表示可能设定的形状；or 连接三个补语。
shaped like Texas 修饰 something。

sentences.sentence: Over time, as baseball evolves, ABS can allow the zone to change with it."
sentences.translation: 随着时间推移，随着棒球运动发展，ABS可以让好球区随之变化。”

structure.text: ABS can allow the zone to change with it
the zone to change with it

difficult_points: allow + 宾语 + to do 表示允许好球区改变；it 指 baseball。
as 从句表示伴随运动发展。

sentences.sentence: "In the past twenty years, sports have moved away from judgment calls.
sentences.translation: “过去20年，体育运动已经逐渐远离人工判断。”

structure.text: have moved away from judgment calls

difficult_points: 现在完成时与 in the past twenty years 表示近20年的趋势。
move away from 表示逐渐摆脱。

sentences.sentence: Soccer has Video Assistant Referees (for offside decisions, for example).
sentences.translation: 足球有视频助理裁判（例如用于越位判定）。
difficult_points: has 表示足球运动采用这种技术。
括号说明视频裁判的具体用途。

sentences.sentence: Tennis has Hawk-Eye (for line calls, for example).
sentences.translation: 网球有鹰眼系统（例如用于界内外判定）。
difficult_points: line calls 指球是否落在线内的判定。
句式与上一句平行，列举不同运动技术。

sentences.sentence: For almost a decade, baseball has used instant replay on the base paths.
sentences.translation: 近十年来，棒球一直在垒线上使用即时回放。

structure.text: instant replay on the base paths

difficult_points: has used 与 for almost a decade 表示持续使用。
on the base paths 限定回放适用的跑垒场景。

sentences.sentence: This is widely liked, even if the precision can sometimes cause problems.
sentences.translation: 这受到广泛欢迎，即使这种精确有时会造成问题。

structure.text: even if the precision can sometimes cause problems

difficult_points: is liked 是被动语态；even if 引导让步从句。
This 指 instant replay。

sentences.sentence: But these applications deal with something physical: bases, lines, goals.
sentences.translation: 但这些技术处理的是实体对象：垒、线和球门。
difficult_points: deal with 表示处理对象；冒号后列举实体。
physical 与后文抽象的好球区形成对比。

sentences.sentence: The boundaries of action are precise, delineated like the keys of a piano.
sentences.translation: 这些行动边界很精确，像钢琴琴键一样清晰划定。

structure.text: delineated like the keys of a piano

difficult_points: delineated 是过去分词补充说明 boundaries 如何被划定。
like 引出钢琴琴键的比喻。

sentences.sentence: This is not the case with ABS and the strike zone.
sentences.translation: ABS和好球区并非如此。

structure.text: is not the case with ABS and the strike zone

difficult_points: be not the case with 表示某情况不适用于某对象。
This 指边界清晰、物理可测的特点。

sentences.sentence: Historically, a certain discretion has been appreciated."
sentences.translation: 从历史上看，一定程度的裁量一直受到珍视。”
difficult_points: 现在完成时被动表示裁量权长期受到认可。
discretion 指裁判根据情境灵活判断的空间。

sentences.sentence: I decided to email Alva Noë, a professor at Berkeley University and a baseball fan, for his opinion.
sentences.translation: 我决定给伯克利大学教授、棒球迷Alva Noë发邮件征求意见。

structure.text: a professor at Berkeley University and a baseball fan

difficult_points: decide to email 表示决定发邮件；同位语补充身份。
for his opinion 表示征求意见的目的。

sentences.sentence: "Hardly a day goes by that I don't wake up and run through the reasons that this [robo-umpires] is such a terrible idea," he replied.
sentences.translation: “几乎没有一天过去而我不起床思考为什么这（机器人裁判）是如此糟糕的想法。”他回答。

structure.text: Hardly a day goes by that I don't wake up and run through the reasons that this [robo-umpires] is such a terrible idea
that I don't wake up and run through the reasons
that this [robo-umpires] is such a terrible idea

difficult_points: Hardly...that... 表示几乎每天都发生；两个 that 从句层层修饰 reasons。
run through 表示快速梳理、思考。

sentences.sentence: He later told me, "This is part of a movement to use algorithms to take the hard choices of living out of life." Perhaps he's right.
sentences.translation: 后来他告诉我：“这是利用算法把生活中的艰难选择排除在生活之外的一场运动。”也许他说得对。

structure.text: This is part of a movement to use algorithms to take the hard choices of living out of life
to use algorithms to take the hard choices of living out of life

difficult_points: to use...to take 两层不定式说明 movement 的目的。
take choices out of life 是比喻，表示排除生活中的艰难选择。

sentences.sentence: We watch baseball to kill time, not to maximize it.
sentences.translation: 我们看棒球是为了消磨时间，而不是把时间利用到最大化。

structure.text: to kill time, not to maximize it

difficult_points: to kill 与 to maximize 构成对比目的；it 指 time。
kill time 是固定表达，不是字面杀死时间。

sentences.sentence: Some players I have met take a dissenting stance toward the robots too, believing that accuracy is not the answer.
sentences.translation: 我遇到的一些球员也对机器人持反对立场，认为准确性并不是答案。

structure.text: take a dissenting stance toward the robots too
believing that accuracy is not the answer
that accuracy is not the answer

difficult_points: I have met 是省略关系代词的定语从句；believing 分词说明立场依据。
the answer 是比喻，表示准确性不能解决所有问题。

sentences.sentence: According to Joe Russo, who plays for a New Jersey team, "With technology, people just want everything to be perfect.
sentences.translation: 为新泽西球队效力的Joe Russo说：“有了技术，人们就只想让一切完美。

structure.text: who plays for a New Jersey team
With technology, people just want everything to be perfect

difficult_points: who 修饰 Joe Russo；with technology 表示有了技术这一条件。
want + 宾语 + to be 结构表示希望一切处于某状态。

sentences.sentence: That's not reality.
sentences.translation: 那不是现实。”
difficult_points: That 指上一句“让一切完美”的愿望。
reality 与 perfect 形成现实和理想的对比。

sentences.sentence: I think perfect would be weird.
sentences.translation: 我觉得完美反而会很奇怪。
difficult_points: perfect 是形容词名词化，作从句主语。
would be 表示假设情境下的判断。

sentences.sentence: Your teams are always winning, work is always just great, there's always money in your pocket, your car never breaks down.
sentences.translation: 你的球队总在获胜，工作总是顺利，口袋里总有钱，汽车也从不抛锚。

structure.text: there's always money in your pocket

difficult_points: 四个分句并列，描绘不现实的“完美生活”。
there is 结构表示口袋里总有钱；break down 指抛锚。

sentences.sentence: What is there to talk about?"
sentences.translation: 那还有什么可谈的呢？”
difficult_points: What is there to... 是询问“有什么可……的”。
to talk about 是不定式后置修饰 what，about 置于句末。

expressions.text: What is there to talk about?
